title: bradleia dioica
intro: Bradleia dioica, also known as the "Mahoe" or "White Hibiscus", is a flowering plant native to Hawaii and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and flowers of the Bradleia dioica plant are said to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal and health benefits.
